metadata.summary: GCP Cloud Run and Cloud Functions Gen2 best practices: secure service deployment, VPC connector, blue-green traffic splitting, Secret Manager integration, and Cloud Run security/setup checklists.

content.title: GCP Cloud Run and Serverless
content.summary: Cloud Run and Cloud Functions Gen2 best practices: deploy with a dedicated service account, Secret Manager secrets, VPC connector for private resource access, min-instances for production cold-start elimination, blue-green traffic splitting for safe rollouts, and Binary Authorization for image trust.

why.p: Cloud Run's default compute service account has broad project-level permissions. Using a dedicated per-service service account limits blast radius. VPC connector is required for private Cloud SQL, Redis, or internal service access — without it, Cloud Run can only reach public endpoints. Blue-green traffic splitting allows canary validation before full cutover without downtime.

list.item: Deploying a containerized stateless API or web application on Cloud Run.
Setting up Cloud Functions Gen2 for HTTP triggers or Pub/Sub event processing.
Configuring blue-green or canary releases for a Cloud Run service.
Adding VPC connector to reach private Cloud SQL or internal services.
Auditing Cloud Run service security configuration.
Stateful workloads or long-running background jobs — use Compute Engine or GKE.
Workloads requiring DaemonSets, host network access, or custom node config — use GKE Standard.
VPC and firewall configuration — use gcp-networking-vpc.

rule.statement: Cloud Run services in production MUST use: a dedicated service account (not the default compute SA), Secret Manager for all credentials, VPC connector with --vpc-egress=all-traffic for private resource access, --no-allow-unauthenticated, and --min-instances=1 to eliminate cold starts. Set --execution-environment=gen2.
rule.rationale: The default compute service account has project-wide Editor-level permissions. Dedicated per-service SAs limit blast radius. VPC egress all-traffic routes all outbound through the VPC, preventing data from bypassing VPC-SC perimeters. Min-instances=1 eliminates the first-request latency spike in production.

example.title: VPC connector for private resource access
example.code:
# Create VPC connector (requires Serverless VPC Access API) gcloud compute networks vpc-access connectors create my-connector \ --region=us-central1 \ --network=my-vpc \ --range=10.8.0.0/28 \ --min-instances=2 \ --max-instances=10
# Deploy with VPC connector gcloud run deploy my-app \ --image=us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/my-project/my-repo/my-app:latest \ --region=us-central1 \ --vpc-connector=my-connector \ --vpc-egress=all-traffic \ --set-env-vars=DB_HOST=10.0.0.5

example.title: Blue-green deployment with tag-based traffic splitting
example.code:
# Deploy new revision without traffic (tagged green) gcloud run deploy my-app \ --image=us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/my-project/my-repo/my-app:v2.0.0 \ --region=us-central1 \ --tag=green \ --no-traffic
# Test green deployment via tag URL curl https://green---my-app-abc123.run.app/health
# Gradually shift 10% traffic to green gcloud run services update-traffic my-app \ --region=us-central1 \ --to-tags=green=10
# Full cutover gcloud run services update-traffic my-app \ --region=us-central1 \ --to-tags=green=100
# Rollback to previous if needed gcloud run services update-traffic my-app \ --region=us-central1 \ --to-latest

rule.statement: Use --no-traffic + tagged revisions for all Cloud Run production deployments. Validate the tagged revision before shifting traffic. Keep the previous revision available for immediate rollback via --to-latest.
rule.rationale: --no-traffic prevents new code from receiving production load until validated. Tags give a stable URL for testing without traffic. --to-latest rollback takes effect in seconds without redeployment.

section.name: cloud-functions-gen2

example.title: Cloud Functions Gen2 — HTTP and Pub/Sub triggers
example.code:
# Deploy HTTP function (Gen2 runs on Cloud Run) gcloud functions deploy my-function \ --gen2 \ --runtime=python312 \ --region=us-central1 \ --source=. \ --entry-point=handler \ --trigger-http \ --service-account=my-function@my-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com \ --set-secrets=API_KEY=api-key:latest \ --memory=256Mi \ --timeout=60s \ --min-instances=0 \ --max-instances=100 \ --no-allow-unauthenticated
# Deploy Pub/Sub triggered function gcloud functions deploy process-messages \ --gen2 \ --runtime=python312 \ --region=us-central1 \ --source=. \ --entry-point=process \ --trigger-topic=my-topic \ --service-account=processor@my-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com

section.note: Cloud Functions Gen2 runs on Cloud Run infrastructure. All Cloud Run best practices (dedicated SA, Secret Manager, VPC connector) apply to Gen2 functions.

checklist.check: Use dedicated service account (not default compute SA).
Enable VPC connector for private resource access.
Configure IAM for authentication (--no-allow-unauthenticated in prod).
Use Secret Manager for sensitive config.
Enable Binary Authorization for trusted images.
Use custom service accounts with minimal permissions.
